frozen_components: MLP and SAE parameters (frozen during decision layer finetuning)
architecture.summary: XNNTab combines an MLP backbone that learns non-linear latent representations with a sparse autoencoder (SAE) that decomposes those representations into monosemantic dictionary features. The SAE encoder projects the MLP's penultimate layer activations into a sparse hidden layer; the decoder reconstructs these activations. A rule-based classifier (Skope-rules) then assigns human-interpretable semantics to each dictionary feature by learning simple decision rules for training instances that highly activate each feature. Finally, the decoder weights and the MLP's decision layer weights are multiplied and combined into a single linear layer, making predictions a direct linear combination of the interpretable monosemantic features.

biblio.title: XNNTab -- Interpretable Neural Networks for Tabular Data using Sparse Autoencoders

claims.statement: XNNTab outperforms interpretable predictive models, and achieves comparable performance to its non-interpretable counterparts.
XNNTab outperforms interpretable models on nearly all datasets, with the exception of CR, for which XNNTab is on par with Logistic Regression.
On GE, XNNTab outperforms all models under comparison.

claims.dataset: Adult, Churn Modeling, Credit_g, Spambase, Covertype, Gesture, Car
claims.statement: The complexity of the rules is on average 2.4 for all datasets.
Learning decision rules to explain learned features is post-hoc and not always complete, as it is not always possible to extract rules for all features.
A high number of learned features results in a high average number of active features per prediction, which limits interpretability.
For the Spambase dataset with threshold 70%, the average number of active features required to inspect is 3.
On AD, XNNTab is comparable to MLP and slightly below the performance of XGBoost and Random Forest.
